goal: Add Git AI comparison and launch trust pages

summary.title: Add launch trust, AI-search, and IndexNow support
summary.what: Added Git AI comparison, changelog, pricing, AI PR intent check, AI-readable knowledge endpoints, JSON-LD markup, crawler-specific robots.txt policy, clearer homepage value statements, and IndexNow key hosting plus sitemap-based URL submission.
summary.why: The user wanted Mainline to move beyond ordinary SEO into category SEO, technical docs SEO, AI-search SEO, and distribution SEO before the open-source launch.
summary.user_goal: Make mainline.sh easier for humans, search engines, and AI answer engines to understand, cite, and recrawl quickly.

decisions.chose: Replace homepage eval metric tiles with cold-start product value statements.
decisions.rationale: Visitors without eval context understand 'before edits', 'avoid abandoned paths', and 'repo-owned memory' faster than 0/9 forbidden-list statistics.

decisions.chose: Add Git AI comparison, changelog, pricing, and AI PR intent check pages through the existing capture-page system.
decisions.rationale: These pages strengthen launch trust, clarify differentiation from AI attribution tools, and show a commercial direction without changing the product repo.

decisions.chose: Add JSON-LD for Organization, SoftwareApplication, WebPage, and BlogPosting.
decisions.rationale: Structured data gives search and AI systems a machine-readable description of the product, pages, and blog post while preserving normal static HTML.

decisions.chose: Expose static AI knowledge endpoints under /api/profile, /api/projects, /api/blog, /api/weekly, and /api/knowledge.
decisions.rationale: The site can provide a compact structured source of truth for AI search without waiting for crawlers to infer everything from separate pages.

decisions.chose: Use robots.txt to explicitly allow search/retrieval crawlers and disallow several training/data crawlers.
decisions.rationale: The user wanted AI search access separated from model-training crawlers so retrieval systems can cite the site while training crawlers are not invited.

decisions.chose: Host the IndexNow key file at the site root and add npm scripts for dry-run and live submissions.
decisions.rationale: IndexNow requires a public key file and a POST payload; keeping it as a repeatable script makes future page launches easy to resubmit.

rejected.reason: The key must be publicly reachable at the declared keyLocation before search engines can validate the submission.
The user correctly pointed out that the 0/9/Git block was unclear for people seeing Mainline for the first time.
The current Astro static deployment can reliably ship static JSON endpoints now; live GitHub data can be added later if the site gets an ISR/server path.

risks: New Chinese and Spanish homepage copy was adjusted during this work and should still receive native-speaker review before high-traffic launch.

acknowledged_constraints.note: New capture pages remain English-only via availableLocales while existing localized homepage copy was updated directly.
The existing native-speaker review concern remains applicable to Chinese and Spanish copy.
Homepage and social card copy now use 'Repo memory before the diff' instead of the stronger stop-language.

review_notes: Production deploy succeeded and https://mainline.sh was aliased to the new build.
Verified the IndexNow key file at https://mainline.sh/5a2a1571c364416eb5f3e5add3653998.txt.
Verified /api/profile returns content-type application/json; charset=utf-8 after adding Vercel headers.
Ran npm run indexnow after deployment; it submitted 64 URLs and received HTTP status 202 from api.indexnow.org.

Bing Webmaster Tools, Google Search Console URL inspection, Perplexity publisher program, llms.txt directory submissions, and GitHub llms-txt-hub PRs still require account or manual form actions outside this repo.

architectural_claims: mainline.sh now exposes static AI-readable JSON endpoints under /api/*.
BaseLayout and ArticleLayout emit JSON-LD structured data for pages and blog posts.
IndexNow submission is implemented as a local Node script driven by public/sitemap.xml plus AI-readable URLs.
behavioral_changes: Homepage proof tiles now explain the product in plain terms instead of showing eval-only statistics.
robots.txt explicitly allows major search/retrieval crawlers and disallows several training/data crawlers.
llms.txt and llms-full.txt include AI knowledge endpoint links.
Vercel sends JSON content-type headers for /api/* routes.
The IndexNow key file is served at the root and npm run indexnow submits 64 URLs.
security_implications: No user data collection, forms, analytics, cookies, or authentication were added.
The IndexNow key is intentionally public per the IndexNow ownership-verification protocol.
migration_notes: Future launch pages can be submitted with npm run indexnow after deployment.
If live GitHub stars/releases are needed later, the static AI knowledge endpoints should move to a server/ISR path instead of pretending static data is live.

resolves_risks.rationale: The homepage and generated social card no longer use the earlier strong 'Stop AI agents...' hero-style copy; they now lead with repo memory before the diff.
